Deck the Halls (2006)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $35,093,569Details
International Box Office $11,722,238
Worldwide Box Office $46,815,807
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $30,537,259
Total $30,537,259
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Steve, suburban dad and Christmas enthusiast, finds a wrinkle in his well-ordered existence with the arrival of his new neighbor Danny. Danny has big dreams and plans to illuminate his house with enough holiday lights to make it visible from space. Not to be outdone, Steve declares a war of one-upmanship with Danny that threatens to drag the Christmas spirit through the slush.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,001,256 (34.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.92 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:75.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$51,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,205 opening theaters/3,205 max. theaters, 4.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $64,049,508

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 22nd, 2006 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
Video Release: November 6th, 2007 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some crude and suggestive humor, and for language.
Running Time: 93 minutes
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family
Release detail: Christmas in November
Social setting: Christmas
Subgenre: Family Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Regency Enterprises, New Regency, Corduroy Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Steve, suburban dad and Christmas enthusiast, finds a wrinkle in his well-ordered existence with the arrival of his new neighbor Danny. Danny has big dreams and plans to illuminate his house with enough holiday lights to make it visible from space. Not to be outdone, Steve declares a war of one-upmanship with Danny that threatens to drag the Christmas spirit through the slush.
